QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_9

question.text: Ms. Vandana has a speculative business loss of Rs.4,00,000 in FY 2025-26. She also has the following incomes: Non-speculative business profit Rs.6,00,000, Salary income Rs.3,00,000, and Short-term capital gain Rs.1,50,000. Against which income can she set off the speculative loss?

answer.text: Against non-speculative business profit of Rs.6,00,000
Against both non-speculative business profit and short-term capital gain
Against any income except salary — so against business profit, STCG, and any other income
Against NONE of these — speculative loss can only be set off against speculative business profit, which she does not have

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Speculative business loss can ONLY be set off against speculative business profit. It cannot be set off against non-speculative business income, salary, capital gains, or any other head. Since Vandana has no speculative profit, the entire Rs.4,00,000 must be carried forward for up to 4 years, to be set off only against speculative profit. **The Trap:** Option A is the strongest distractor — many candidates assume speculative and non-speculative business incomes are under the same head (Profits & Gains of Business or Profession), so intra-head set-off should work. But speculative loss has a special restriction. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) tests speculative vs. non-speculative income classification for F&O trading. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clients engaged in speculative trading (intraday equity) should understand that losses can only absorb future speculative profits — they offer no relief against other income streams.
